2604.12442.title: GLeMM: A large-scale multilingual dataset for morphological research
2604.12442.paper_summary: In derivational morphology, what mechanisms govern the variation in form-meaning relations between words? The answers to this type of questions are typically based on intuition and on observations drawn from limited data, even when a wide range of languages is considered. Many of these studies are difficult to replicate and generalize. To address this issue, we present GLeMM, a new derivational resource designed for experimentation and data-driven description in morphology. GLeMM is characterized by (i) its large size, (ii) its extensive coverage (currently amounting to seven European languages, i.e., German, English, Spanish, French, Italian, Polish, Russian, (iii) its fully automated design, identical across all languages, (iv) the automatic annotation of morphological features on each entry, as well as (v) the encoding of semantic descriptions for a significant subset of these entries. It enables researchers to address difficult questions, such as the role of form and meaning in word-formation, and to develop and experimentally test computational methods that identify the structures of derivational morphology. The article describes how GLeMM is created using Wiktionary articles and presents various case studies illustrating possible applications of the resource.
2604.12442.paper_summary_zh: 在派生形態學中，什麼機制主導了單詞之間形式-意義關係的變化？這類問題的答案通常基於直覺和從有限數據中得出的觀察，即使考慮了廣泛的語言範圍。許多這些研究難以複製和概括。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了 GLeMM，一個旨在進行實驗和數據驅動描述的全新派生資源。GLeMM 的特點包括 (i) 大型規模，(ii) 廣泛的覆蓋範圍（目前涵蓋七種歐洲語言，即德語、英語、西班牙語、法語、意大利語、波蘭語、俄語），(iii) 完全自動化的設計，在所有語言中一致，(iv) 對每個條目的形態特徵進行自動標註，以及 (v) 對這些條目中重要子集的語義描述進行編碼。它使研究人員能夠解決困難的問題，例如形式和意義在詞形成中的作用，以及開發和實驗測試識別派生形態學結構的計算方法。本文描述了如何使用維基詞典文章創建 GLeMM，並展示了多個案例研究，說明該資源的可能應用。
2604.12442.author: Hathout Nabil et.al.
2604.12442.authors: Hathout Nabil, Basilio Calderone, Fiammetta Namer, Franck Sajous
